reasoning: The climate.mil portal was taken offline by the Trump administration shortly after taking office in January 2025, alongside widespread removals of federal climate data. While the USDA recently committed to restoring some of its own purged climate webpages in response to a lawsuit from farmers (who could demonstrate direct standing and reliance on the data), there is no evidence of a similar lawsuit to compel the Department of Defense to restore the climate.mil domain. Furthermore, members of the public are unlikely to have standing to sue for the restoration of a military planning portal. Since the site remains down as of early May 2025 and is controlled strictly by the DoD (a .mil domain), it will only come back online if the administration voluntarily reverses its own policy—which is extremely contrary to their documented agenda—or is forced to by a fast-moving court order, which hasn't even been filed yet. It's highly improbable the site will return by July 1, 2025.
